channel.title: Latest Finextra Research Artificial intelligence Headlines
channel.description: The Latest Financial IT Artificial intelligence Headlines

item.title: Moody’s warns slow AI adoption could erode margins and market share
item.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer just a technology; it’s a credit risk. That’s the message from Moody’s Ratings in its latest update to the AI Corporate Heatmap, which assesses the impact of AI advances on corporate creditworthiness through 2030.

item.title: Feedzai hits $2bn valuation
item.description: AI-based financial crime detection platform Feedzai has hit a $2 billion valuation off the back of a $75 million funding round.

item.title: Sibos 2025: BBH’s Adrian Whelan on overcoming obstacles in ESG and DEI policy
item.description: At Sibos 2025 in Frankfurt, Finextra spoke to Adrian Whelan, global head of market intelligence at Brown Brothers Harriman (BBH), on data scarcity, environmental, social, and governance (ESG) investing, how the industry is handling diversity and inclusion in the current political landscape.

item.title: Wealth app Chip scores £6m investment from Channel 4 VC arm
item.description: Chip has secured its first institutional investment, with the venture capital unit of UK TV station Channel 4 pumping £6 million into the digital wealth app at a £208 million valuation.

item.title: Sibos 2025: DeFi expected to take over financial ecosystem
item.description: In the rapidly developing financial universe, there are certain buzzwords that have been reappearing throughout discussions: blockchain, stablecoins, and tokenisation. While cryptocurrencies are not new, these new developments in decentralised finance (DeFi) are taking the financial sector by storm.

item.title: ECB turns to AI startup for fraud detection for digital euro
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has selected Portugal-based startup Feedzai to provide fraud detection and prevention for its digital euro.

item.title: Proptech firm Baselane raises $34m in funding
item.description: Baselane, a fintech platform designed for real estate investors, is set to expand its operations after securing $34m in funding.

item.title: BBVA lets customers use Apple AI to create their own card designs
item.description: BBVA and Visa are letting customers use their iPhones' AI-powered Image Playground feature to personalise their payment cards.

item.title: Sibos 2025: Cybersecurity, AI and quantum dominate industry panel
item.description: At Sibos 2025 in Frankfurt, experts spanning multinational banks and fintechs explored the emergence of sophisticated cyberthreats. Bad actors are utilising AI, quantum, and machine learning to level up their attacks, but can financial institutions fight fire with fire? The panel ‘Navigating the intersection of technology and financial security’ explored how disruptive technologies will impact the fraud landscape.

item.title: PayOS and Mastercard complete milestone agentic payment transaction
item.description: PayOS and Mastercard have completed an autonomos agentic payment transaction using a Mastercard Agentic Token.

item.title: Sibos 2025: How to build the bank of tomorrow
item.description: Following the popular session on building tomorrow’s bank at Sibos 2024, McKinsey’s Megha Kansal moderated a follow-up session on the year’s developments in Frankfurt.

item.title: Bank of America rolls out GenAI assistant for Global Payment Solutions business
item.description: Bank of America has rolled out an AI assistant to help staff deal with complex queries from institutional users of its Global Payments Solutions product.

item.title: 69% of CEOs to allocate over 10% of budgets to AI over the next 12 months
item.description: CEO confidence in the global economy has hit a five-year low, according to the KPMG 2025 Global CEO Outlook, as corporate leaders focus strategic investments in AI, talent and risk resilience to sustain and fuel future growth.

item.title: BGC Group acquires MAcro Hive
item.description: BGC Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: BGC), a leading global brokerage and financial technology company, today announced the acquisition of Macro Hive Limited (“Macro Hive”), a leading provider of global macro market analytics and strategy.

item.title: Bud launches MCP server for AI-driven banking
item.description: Bud Financial (“Bud”), the AI platform that turns banking data into actionable insights, today announced the release of the Bud MCP server, enabling rapid development of AI agents that benefit from a deep, real-time understanding of users’ finances.

item.title: Irish startup Leapifai launches digital tax copilot
item.description: Leapifai, a pioneering Irish fintech start-up, today announced the launch of its novel digital tax copilot platform, designed to modernise tax compliance to create efficiencies that allow accountants as well as financial advisors to serve more clients.

item.title: Communify adds AI-powered Stories apps to scale personalisation
item.description: Communify, the leading provider of Financial AI, Digital and Data solutions for the financial industry, today announced the expansion of its flagship MIND AI Stories offering to include robust client-level data.

item.title: Carta buys AI-powered data automation platform Accelex
item.description: Carta, the software platform purpose-built for private capital, today announced it has acquired Accelex, the AI-powered data automation platform focused on alternative investments for institutional limited partners (LPs).

item.title: NCino unveils mortgage-focused AI innovations
item.description: NCino, Inc. (NASDAQ: NCNO), the leading provider of intelligent, best-in-class banking solutions, today announced a new wave of mortgage-focused AI innovations designed to accelerate loan origination, reduce underwriting touches and deliver more responsive borrower journeys.

item.title: Outdated payments systems driving customers away - survey
item.description: FreedomPay, a global leader in Next Level Commerce™, and Stripe, the programmable financial services company, today released the "Enterprise Payments Playbook," a new report in partnership with Forbes Insights.

item.title: Nuvei introduces AI integration agent
item.description: Nuvei announced the launch of its AI-powered Integration Agent, a new solution that enables merchants and partners to connect to its global payments infrastructure in hours instead of weeks.

item.title: Attivo deploys Recordsure’s AI technology
item.description: Attivo, a forward-thinking and rapidly growing independent financial advice firm, has implemented Recordsure’s predictive AI technology to enhance its ongoing advice compliance, ensure consistent and compliant service delivery, and reduce the cost of file reviews.

item.title: Nivo achieves ISO 42001 certification for AI
item.description: Nivo has become one of the first financial services organisations in the UK to achieve ISO 42001 certification – the new international standard for Artificial Intelligence Management Systems (AIMS).

item.title: Türk Ekonomi Bankası selects Provenir AI Decisioning Platform
item.description: Provenir, a global leader in AI risk decisioning software, today announced Türk Ekonomi Bankası (TEB) has selected the Provenir AI Decisioning Platform to speed batch risk decisioning processes for its retail and small business lending products.

item.title: How Structured Data is Key to Leveraging AI in Core Banking
item.description: Joining the virtual studio for a FinextraTV interview, Barb Morgan, Chief Product and Technology Officer, Temenos helps to shine a light on how artificial intelligence is helping to evolve core banking processes. Discussing how these AI boosts have been implemented to keep up with evolving customer expectations, Morgan provided insights into how banks are focussing investment into having clean, high quality structured data.

item.title: AI Adoption is Rising, But is Everyone Succeeding?
item.description: Following the continual rise in AI Adoption, FinextraTV spoke to Ioan Iacob, Founder and CEO, FlowX.AI to discuss the ways people have been following this adoption and answer the crucial question: are we seeing the successes to match the popularity? Mentioning the challenges of embedding AI into existing systems, describing the true benefits of Agentic AI in particular and even quoting Pink Floyd's "you can't have your pudding, if you dont eat your meat", Iacob looks towards the future of adoption and usage.

item.title: Why Data-Driven Fraud Calls for Smarter Solutions
item.description: Discussing the impact of AI-generated fraud threats across the digital world, Laura Quevedo, Executive Vice President, Fraud and Decisioning Solutions at Mastercard, joined the FinextraTV studio at their RiskX event in Rome. Quevedo also explained how AI can fight against it's fraud counterparts, organisations need greater, more detailed transaction information to match the sophistication of modern fraud threats.

item.title: Can Open Finance & Agentic AI Enable a Future of Financial Freedom?
item.description: Discussing the role of Agentic AI and Open Finance in the future of financial services, Jess Turner, Executive Vice President, Global Head of Open Finance and Developer Experience, Mastercard joined the FinextraTV studio at Mastercard's RiskX event in Rome. Turner explains the importance of creating a comfortable developer experience to enable solutions that give AI agents autonomy to act safely and securely with consumers towards a freer, open finance future. As part of this, she describes the key challenges to overcome from data governance to agentic discoverability and consumer consent.

item.title: AI Innovation: Balancing Trust, Agility and Speed in Banking.
item.description: In this FinextraTV interview, Ian Watterson, SVP Go-To-Market at CSG, shares how banks can bring AI innovation into everyday operations without losing customer trust. He explores the paradox of moving fast while staying agile—and why getting it right is critical for both corporate and retail banking.

item.title: The Importance of AI with Privacy at Its Core
item.description: Taking time out from Mastercard's RiskX event in Rome, Caroline Louveaux, Chief Privacy, AI & Data Responsibility Officer at Mastercard, joined the FinextraTV studio to talk about how to embed privacy into design solutions. Beyond this, Louveaux spoke to the use of synthetic data and called for more proactive governance, instead of reactive compliance.

item.title: How the Future of AI Will Be a Single Flow of Agentic Commerce
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV studio at RiskX's Mastercard event in Rome, Greg Ulrich, Chief AI and Data Officer at Mastercard described what the future of commerce looks like amongst the backdrop of an AI revolution. Ulrich outlines the evolution of a single flow of commerce, enabled by semi-autonomous agents, and Mastercard’s role to use AI to make commerce smarter, safer, more personal and its operations stronger.

item.title: The Future of Digital Commerce: Preparing for an always-on, AI-powered world
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV studio at Mastercard's RiskX event in Rome, Craig Vosburg, Chief Services Officer at Mastercard discussed how a global economy that thrives on interconnected devices and AI is ripe for increasingly sophisticated cyber threats, and how organizations can prepare for this future with an always-on security approach and effective partnerships.

item.title: Specialisation, AI, and the Fight Against All-Cause Fraud
item.description: In this FinextraTV interview, John Filby, CEO of Outseer, emphasizes the power of specialisation in the AI-driven fight against fraud. With scams and authorized fraud escalating, Filby outlined how organisations must turn to “all-cause” fraud prevention platforms, like their own, that can leverage all forms of AI for greater, more intentional protection. He also highlights how anchoring to a higher purpose, in this case, partnering with banks to "make the world safer" will be key to long-term protection.

item.title: Navigating the Crucial Differences Between Public and Private AI
item.description: Helping to chart the evolution of AI across the years, from automated systems like algorithmic trading to more modern generative and agentic versions today, John Trapani, Industry Leader, Financial Services, Appian, recently joined the FinextraTV studio. Reflecting on this history, Trapani questions how important it is for organisations to understand the regulatory and privacy differences between public and private, as well as asserting his belief that we are still in a phase of needing to babysit the AI systems.

item.title: Securing the Future: The Role of Collaboration in Protecting the Digital World
item.description: Stepping away from Mastercard's RiskX event, Johan Gerber, Global Head of Security Solutions at Mastercard joined the FinextraTV studio to discuss the importance of securing the digital ecosystem. Gerber discusses the complex balance between innovation and risk mitigation, and the importance of protecting the small businesses that play an outsized role in the global economy.

item.title: What is Fueling Impactful AI Solutions for the Australian Market?
item.description: Whilst at the Communify Insights Experience in London, Will Bailey, President, International Sales, Communify Fincentric joined the FinextraTV studio to describe how Knowledge Bases help financial firms understand and trust their data sources. Speaking to his base location of Australia, Bailey shares how Australians, in particular, are struggling with managing data governance and how compliant, trusted sources of data are far safer and more insightful than simply relying on casual AI usage.

item.title: How Key Trends Are Redefining Wealth Management in 2025
item.description: Attending the Communify Insights Experience in London, Rupert Baron, Former CEO, Investment Management, Rathbones joined the FinextraTV studio and gave his perspective on what 'wealth' actually means in an industry of growing complexity. Defining wealth as a merge of investment and financial planning, Barron provided overviews on consolidation, private equity and AI, what it is that has driven such change, and how firms can move forward in the modern era.

item.title: Modernising for what’s next: Data-led innovation in financial services
item.description: AI is here – but is the industry’s data ready?

item.title: What’s next in wealth? Exploring European trends & priorities
item.description: How can wealth managers best serve the next generation of investors?

item.title: Growing pains: Evolving core banking for an age of AI
item.description: From aging foundations to digital-first banking and AI.

item.title: How smart FIs are achieving their AI transformation targets
item.description: Discover the most successful AI use cases in banking.

item.title: GenAI and data management: The next-generation approach to customer experience
item.description: Why should FIs implement GenAI across their data processing flows?

item.title: Consumer lending’s future: When embedded finance meets smarter decisioning
item.description: What role does AI-driven decisioning play in enabling smarter, fairer, and faster credit approvals? How can the industry ensure transparency and accountability in automated lending?

item.title: From Data Deluge to AI Advantage: Prioritising Resilience, Security, and Exceptional CX
item.description: How can omnichannel personalisation and real-time feedback loops enable digital experiences that are powered by search-powered customer journey insights, APM, and behavioural analytics?

item.title: AI-powered banking stacks: The secret to fraud management
item.description: What does an AI bank of the future look like?

item.title: PREDICT 2026: New financial fabric - Digital assets, smart infrastructure, global resilience
item.description: How are central banks and financial institutions leveraging AI and quantum technologies to future-proof digital asset infrastructure and policy frameworks?

item.title: AI agents meet stablecoins…when money gets interesting!
item.description: Imagine waking up and… Your alarm goes off at 6am. By the time your feet hit the floor, an AI agen

item.title: ESG And AI: the reality behind the myth
item.description: AI is often positioned as the force that will transform ESG analysis. Advocates present it as the te...

item.title: Zero Trust Auditing: Redefining Assurance for the AI-Era Enterprise
item.description: The End of Perimeter Thinking For decades, audit and security teams have relied on a familiar map: u...

item.title: Reuters Momentum Highlights: Leading Through the Acceleration of AI
item.description: I had the opportunity to moderate a panel at the Reuters Momentum AI event in London this week, wher...

item.title: Data-Led Innovation with AI: Transforming Financial Services
item.description: As the financial services industry is undergoing a seismic shift, driven by the exponential growth

item.title: Sponsor's message: [Webinar] Modernising for what’s next: Data-led Innovation in Financial Services
item.description: [Webinar] Modernising for what’s next: Data-led Innovation in Financial Services
